groups.title: Earth-sheltered Housing
groups.desc:
Questions 31-36
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
groups.collect: Advantages: • has a $31$ on the environment • increased use of energy-efficient $32$ technologies • can have a very long $33$ • easy to maintain • no noise from the $34$ outside (a big problem in many conventional homes) • health benefits from advanced methods of $35$ Earth-sheltered housing in the UK: • no longer difficult to get planning permission • more likely to get approval for development in $36$ areas • may replace mobile homes on the coast

questions.title: no noise from the 【   】 outside (a big problem in many conventional homes)
health benefits from advanced methods of 【   】
more likely to get approval for development in 【   】 areas

groups.question_title: Which UK projects have the following features?
groups.desc:
Questions 37-40
Which UK projects have the following features?
Write the correct letter, A, B or C, next to Questions 37-40.

transcript.en: For my presentation today, I will focus on earth-sheltered housing or underground housing as it's more commonly known.
I'll illustrate how it can meet the demands of ecological environmental planning regarding building and energy resources.
Let's first look at the advantages of earth-sheltered housing.
Perhaps the most significant merit of earth-sheltered housing is the low impact on the environment since houses built underground can hardly be seen from the surface.
Then, earth-sheltered houses are not only environmentally friendly but also surprisingly efficient regarding energy.
It is because underground housing can make great use of the alternative technologies to improve energy efficiency.
Moreover, many earth-sheltered housing projects use natural, non-toxic building materials.
Some are even more durable than standard building materials.
As a result, earth-sheltered houses have a lifespan of 500 years or more.

This type of building technique means less exposure to the outside elements like sunshine, storm, snow etc., so it can give the houses actual protection and keep the maintenance of the houses to the minimum.
Earth-sheltered houses also give people a quiet environment.
As we know, noise is a severe problem in modern society, and many people consider moving home because of this.
In that case, earth-sheltered houses might be a good choice because living underground means those people will no longer suffer from the disturbances caused by the traffic on the road.
And lastly, earth-sheltered houses also bring significant health advantages to people.
Because of the sophistication of the ventilation systems installed in earth-sheltered houses, it provides a comfortable living environment for people who have asthma and other chest diseases.
Then let's move on to earth-sheltered housing in the UK.
Despite all the apparent advantages we mentioned above, earth-sheltered housing still isn't a popular choice for most British families, as the statistics show.
The British Earth-sheltering Association tries to persuade people to try this type of accommodation, saying that it's easier to get planning permission for an earth-sheltered house.
In contrast, for a conventional home, there is always too much red tape.
A lot of evidence shows that the building development offices are more sympathetic to earth-sheltered projects, especially in the conservation areas.
On the sites where conventional houses aren't usually allowed to build, earth-sheltered housing might be a feasible solution.
And there is even a possibility that one day, earth-sheltered houses can take the place of mobile homes along the coastlines in the UK.
In the last part, I'd like to talk about two different projects in the UK: Mole Manor and Hockerton.
When it was first built, Mole Manor attracted wide publicity around the world.
A newspaper poll conducted by the Earth-sheltering Association showed that Mole Manor was voted as one of the twelve most desirable places to live in the world.
Similarly, Hockerton aroused considerable interest from the international media as well, winning several awards for its innovative environmental design.
The running cost for Mole Manor is much lower than that of conventional houses.
It is because Mole Manor is installed with an effective insulation system, significantly reducing its heating bill.
While at Hockerton, all the basic needs of families living there are satisfied by energies in this area.
For example, wind power, solar energy and rain are used to generate enough warmth, water for washing and drinking, and electricity for lighting and cooking.
As a result, people there can live a lifestyle in harmony with the environment.
It is also proved that at Hockerton, earth-sheltered houses can be built on a modest budget because it gives priority to cost when designing the houses.
But it is not the case with Mole Manor, which pays more attention to style than cost.
Compared with the homely cottage style at Hockerton, the houses at Mole Manor are expensive, beautiful and comfortable, and it is said that these houses are worth more than 800,000 pounds today.
In conclusion, Mole Manor and Hockerton Projects show that earth-sheltered housing has numerous advantages.
They can not only provide comfortable and stylish houses to people but are also beneficial to the environment.
And with the help of new energy-saving technology, it can meet all the criteria for ecological and environmental planning.

31.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 advantages，提示词为 a、environment，此处应填单数名词或名词短语。
题目解析：原文开篇有一小段背景介绍，当听到“Let's first look at the advantages of earth-sheltered housing.”时，可以定位到该题。原文提到 the most significant merit，说明答案即将出现，随后听到 the low impact on the environment，对应题干信息，可判断 low impact 为正确答案。

32.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 energy-efficient、technologies，此处应填形容词或名词。
题目解析：上一题结束后，听到逻辑承接词 then 时，可以定位到该题，接着相继听到题干中的 efficient 和 energy，可以准确定位。之后答案句出现“... underground housing can make great use of the alternative technologies to improve energy efficiency”，对应题干信息，可判断 alternative 为正确答案。

33.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 a、long，此处应填单数名词或名词短语。
题目解析：上一题结束后，听到逻辑承接词 moreover 时，可以定位到该题。原文提到“... earth-sheltered houses have a lifespan of 500 years or more.”，其中 500 years or more 是题干 long 的同义替换，可判断 lifespan 为正确答案。

34.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 noise，提示词为 no、the、outside，此处应填名词或名词短语。
题目解析：上一题结束后，定位词 noise 原词重现。原文先提到“... noise is a severe problem in modern society, and many people consider moving home because of this.”，该句对应题干括号内的信息 a big problem in many conventional homes，但并未提及噪音的来源，继续往下听，可听到“... living underground means those people will no longer suffer from the disturbances caused by the traffic on the road.”，其中 no longer 对应题干否定信息，可判断 traffic 为噪音来源，符合题干要求，为正确答案。

35.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 health benefits，提示词为 advanced methods、of，此处应填名词或名词短语。
题目解析：上一题结束后，可以听到逻辑承接词 lastly 以及定位词的同义词 health advantages，接着原文提到“Because of the sophistication of the ventilation systems installed in earth-sheltered houses ...”，其中 sophistication 对应题干的 advanced methods，可判断 ventilation (systems) 为正确答案。注意，本题替换信息的抓取和答案词的拼写难度都比较大。

36.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 planning permission、approval，提示词为 more likely、in、areas，此处应填形容词或名词。
题目解析：通过上层信息 planning permission 辅助定位。上层信息结束后，原文提到“A lot of evidence shows that the building development offices are more sympathetic to earth-sheltered projects, especially in the conservation areas.”，其中 more sympathetic to 对应题干的 more likely to get approval，areas 后所缺内容即 conservation，因此可判断 conservation 为正确答案。

37.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 favourable reaction。
题目解析：应对这组配对题，考生需要注意以下几点：1. 选项的内容不会被替换，题干的内容会被替换，因此需要听取替换信息来定位每一道题；2. 选项的内容虽然会原词出现，但其在原文中是先于题干替换信息出现的，因此在听到选项的项目名称出现时，需要先记录下来（记录项目首字母即可）；3. 选项没有先后顺序，但答案位置的先后顺序与题号顺序基本一致，可帮助定位。本题应对方法是，上一题结束后，原文提到“In the last part, I'd like to talk about two different projects in the UK: Mole Manor and Hockerton.”，可以定位到该组配对题。先听到 Mole Manor，随后听到 attracted wide publicity around the world，对应题干 favourable reaction；接着又听到 Hockerton 和 aroused considerable interest from the international media，对应题干 favourable reaction，可判断 Mole Manor 和 Hockerton 都符合要求，因此 C 选项为正确答案。

38.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 energy，提示词为 self-sufficiency。
题目解析：上一题结束后，原文先提到 Mole Manor，但随后提到的信息 effective insulation system、reducing heating bill 与题干信息不匹配。接着提到 Hockerton，随后提到“... all the basic needs of families living there are satisfied by energies in this area.”，后面还提到 wind power、solar energy、rain、water、electricity 等具体的能源类型，对应题干信息，可判断 B 选项为正确答案。

39.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 low building costs。
题目解析：上一题结束后，原文提到 Hockerton，接着提到 earth-sheltered houses can be built on a modest budget，其中 built on a modest budget 对应题干的 low building costs，因此 B 选项为正确答案。

40.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 luxurious accommodating。
题目解析：上一题结束后，原文提到 Mole Manor，接着提到 pays more attention to style than cost，此时还不能确定答案，需要继续往下听。原文提到“Compared with the homely cottage style at Hockerton ...”，注意句中的 homely cottage style 与 luxurious 的意思相反，可判断 Hockerton 为干扰选项。接着提到“... the houses at Mole Manor are expensive, beautiful and comfortable ... worth more than 800,000 pounds today.”，对应题干 luxurious accommodating，可判断 A 选项为正确答案。
